text:
Great to be featured on MarketWatch discussing $AMZN ~$12B deal for Globalstar.

You can spend billions on satellites but spectrum and licensing are much harder to replicate which is why this deal expands Amazon’s connectivity moat.

SK Hynix has started mass production of its SOCAMM2 memory module for $NVDA Vera Rubin platform.

It offers more than 2x the bandwidth with roughly 75% better power efficiency which is another sign that specialized memory is becoming critical to next-gen AI server design.

$META and $GOOGL now control ~53% of global digital ad spend.

That tells you just how concentrated the internet economy has become.

My top two growth positions seem to be having their own race to $100. 

$RKLB, $AEHR

$META just partnered with CBRE on a new multiyear “LevelUp” program to recruit and train thousands of fiber technicians to help build U.S. data centers.

Scaling AI infrastructure will also require a much larger skilled workforce.

$TSLA launched robotaxi service in Dallas & Houston this weekend & Morgan Stanley said early footage suggests the rides may already be operating without in-car safety monitors.

Another step toward Tesla’s goal of launching in 7 new cities by mid-2026.

$ADBE just announced a new set of AI agents aimed at helping enterprise customers automate digital marketing and other workflows.

Adobe also said it is partnering with 30+ AI platforms and companies including $AMZN, $MSFT, $NVDA, Anthropic and OpenAI.

Tom Lee's $BMNR now holds ~4.12% of all Ethereum.

They added ~102K $ETH last week bringing their total to ~4.98M.

$ONDS received an initial $10M order under a broader $50M Israel border demining award.

Including its separate Syria border project, Ondas now has about $80M in active demining tenders.

$TSLA lithium refinery is built to have a lower carbon footprint than traditional hard rock refining.

It uses an acid-free process, recycles and treats water on site and supports a more closed-loop battery supply chain.

$MU has driven more than half of the S&P 500 EPS revision since the war began.

AI is turning memory into a performance bottleneck making it one of the cleaner toll booths in the stack.

$GOOGL is set to unveil its next generation of custom AI chips (TPUs) at Google Cloud Next this week.

The messaging is squarely around generative AI, infrastructure, security and agentic AI workflows.

THE OPTICAL PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK

As AI clusters scale past copper’s physical limits, the bottleneck shifts to optical & these are the companies building that layer across the stack: 

1. $AAOI building the transceiver layer of the AI network through vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. It has already secured over $200M in its first volume 1.6T order from one hyperscale customer followed by another $124M in 800G orders from a second.

2. $AEHR building the reliability layer for the optical & AI hardware stack through burn-in & test systems. It just received a record $41M follow-on order from its lead hyperscale customer reinforcing the idea that Sonoma is becoming a key production burn-in platform for high-power AI ASICs.

3. $CRDO building the connectivity layer that helps AI clusters move data faster through active electrical cables, retimers & high-speed interconnect silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition also extends that platform into silicon photonics before copper becomes a real constraint.

4. $LITE building the laser layer of the AI optical stack through EMLs, optical components & optical switching exposure. The setup is backed by a $2B $NVDA strategic investment & optical circuit switch backlog above $400M with orders reportedly extending through 2028.

5. $VIAV building the testing & validation layer of the optical stack through network instrumentation & photonics measurement tools. It is the picks-and-shovels layer of the transition because every high-speed optical buildout still needs to be tested regardless of which transceiver vendor wins.

6. $COHR building one of the core photonics bottlenecks through indium phosphide lasers, optical engines & communications components tied to next-gen AI networking. It also has a $2B $NVDA strategic investment behind it & is doubling InP device capacity into the 1.6T ramp.

7. $MRVL building the DSP & optical infrastructure layer through electro-optics, PAM DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom networking chips. The Celestial AI deal & NVLink Fusion exposure both strengthen its position as photonics becomes more central to AI cluster design.

Blue Origin just reminded everyone that launched and delivered are not the same thing.

Let’s see how they make it right for $ASTS because the public tone so far has been a lot more “great flight” than “sorry about the satellite.”

Bit like an airline celebrating the takeoff when the passenger ended up in the wrong city.

$MRVL just hit $150 for the first time ever after a report that $GOOGL is working with Marvell on custom AI silicon for inference.

The reported designs include a memory chip to pair with TPUs and a next-gen TPU which would expand Marvell’s role deeper into Google’s AI stack.

$ASTS is down ~15% after Blue Origin placed BlueBird 7 into an orbit too low to sustain operations despite a successful separation and power-on.

Insurance should cover the loss and the broader New Glenn launch cadence thesis does not change here.

Is the SaaSpocalypse now over?

Watch for a tone change tomorrow morning.

AI startup Fluidstack is in talks to raise $1 billion in capital at an $18 billion valuation, with Jane Street and Situational Awareness in talks to co-lead the round. 

This would more than double Fluidstack’s valuation from $7.5 billion.

$NVDA $CRWV $NBIS

OpenAI is reportedly projecting ad revenue to reach $2.5 billion this year, before scaling to $11 billion by 2027, per Axios, with the company recently beginning to pilot ads to select US customers. 

$MSFT $AMZN $GOOG $META

Alibaba and China Telecom are building an AI data center featuring 10,000 of Alibaba's own Zhenwu AI chips for training and inference, able to handle models with hundreds of billions of parameters. 

$BABA $BIDU $NVDA

Meta is projected to surpass Alphabet in digital ad revenue by the end of this year, per eMarketer, projecting Meta to reach $243.5 billion in ad revenue versus Alphabet’s $239.5 billion.

📺 WHAT TO DO AFTER A 13-DAY HISTORIC RALLY

Trade LIVE with me this week:

🔴secure.t3live.com/alpha-team…

We had one of the strongest V-shaped recoveries ever, with 13 consecutive up days — the longest streak since 1992.

This wasn’t a normal rally. It was fast, aggressive, and driven by positioning (short covering + chasing).

The oscillator hit +82 — unusually high (rare/extreme level). That signals short-term exhaustion and a high probability of digestion or pullback.

However, overbought ≠ immediate crash. It usually means pause, chop, or controlled pullback.

So, prepare for consolidation or a shallow pullback to reset conditions.

*
$SPY is pointing to a slight down opening, but we are still above Friday’s low and have not even filled the gap yet.

That’s actually bullish behavior: weakness is controlled, and no real breakdown yet.

So here is my plan:

I will gradually cover $SPY shorts but not aggressively → carefully unwinding.

I already trimmed my longs heavily last week into strength and now I am watching for re-entry opportunities and select adds if the market stabilizes.

Some stocks in focus:
– $AAPL → strong, holding
– $NVDA → key test
– $TSLA → watching for red-to-green move 
– $AMZN → stretched, possibly rebuy lower
– $IBIT 

*

Watch $QQQ closely today.

– $644 → must-hold level (strength scenario) 
– $635–$636 → breakout retest zone (healthy pullback) 

Scenarios:
1. Hold $644 + go green → shorts stay trapped → continuation higher
2. Break lower → test $635–$636 → normal pullback → still bullish structure
3. Deeper retrace → even better for reloading quality names

*

One important lesson to share: I took $ASTS into the weekend launch, which did not go well.

But I took options rather than shares. Never take shares into binary events, like earnings, FDA decisions, or launches like this.

Why? Options = defined risk (premium), while stock = large capital exposure. Example: $2K risk (options) vs $10K risk (shares).

You will survive by controlling downside, not chasing upside.

*

So, today, don’t commit to one outcome; prepare for continuation, pullback, or chop.

The edge comes from being flexible + position sizing + patience.

This is not the time to be aggressive — it’s the time to be precise and selective.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

Good morning. Mixed markets around the World as the banter continues ahead of more ceasefire talks. Europe down 1.5%. Asia flattish. $Spx futures-38.

I fast regularly. Not because it's easy — because it's training.

Missing a meal when everyone's eating teaches you something markets can't: sitting with discomfort is a skill.

When $SPY hits all-time highs and euphoria peaks, the investor who trained for discomfort wins.

$BTC at $77,501 — riding the risk-on wave.

Up from $73.5K earlier in the week.

But here's what doesn't change with price: Bitcoin mining costs hover near $80K.

Miner stress below cost-of-production is a structural overhang the bulls haven't fully priced in.

Retail investors: if you own bond funds, ask yourself one question. Does your fund change its positioning based on market conditions? If not, consider $JOJO. It does.

At the March 30 low, every major U.S. index was RED for 2026.

$SPY. $QQQ. $IWM. $DIA. All negative.

Four weeks later: all at ALL-TIME HIGHS.

The full reversal from across-the-board red to across-the-board records is historically rare. Don't dismiss it.

$TSLA reports earnings April 22.

That same week: 94 $SPY component companies report.

The busiest earnings week of the year hits when markets are at ALL-TIME HIGHS.

Earnings either validate this rally — or give every reluctant bear a reason to act.

$SLV surged 6.5% in a week to $80.78 — outperforming $GLD.

Silver has BOTH the monetary metal bid AND industrial demand tailwinds.

When silver outperforms gold this aggressively, it's usually telling you something about where the economy is heading.

$GLD at $4,868 per oz — holding near record highs.

Equities at all-time highs. Oil crashing. Risk-on everywhere.

And gold is OUTPERFORMING every major index YTD.

When safe-haven demand persists through a risk-on melt-up, that's a signal worth watching.

$CL_F crashed 10% in a single session — April 17.

WTI fell to $84, down from a $110 peak.

One day. 10%. Oil.

That's not noise — that's a deflationary impulse hitting the macro backdrop at full speed.

$QQQ closed at 24,468 — a new ALL-TIME HIGH.

The Nasdaq just posted its longest positive winning streak since 1992.

That's not a typo. 1992.

Tech is leading this rally — the only question is how long it can sustain it.

$SPY just closed at 7,126 — an ALL-TIME HIGH.

+13% in 11 sessions from the March 30 low.

That's the fastest recovery since 1982.

Every bear who called a prolonged collapse is now underwater.

The S&P just hit a RECORD HIGH. Oil crashed 13% in a week. Iran says the Strait is "completely open." Hapag-Lloyd and Maersk won't send ships through. The ceasefire expires TUESDAY. And the Navy just put 5-inch gun rounds into an Iranian vessel.

leadlagreport.com/p/the-stra…

Banks crushed earnings. Oil crashed 11% in a day. Gold refused to sell off. The Nasdaq just posted its longest winning streak since 1992.

Something has to give.

The ceasefire expires Wednesday.

leadlagreport.com/p/record-h…
